reportText: A fantastic afternoon of bowls was enjoyed by all on a beautifully prepared home green.
The match was tightly contested early on, but our triples clicked into gear during the second half. Dean and Roger picked up crucial shots on the 14th and 15th ends to pull us ahead.
Thank you to everyone who came out to support. Next week we look forward to our away fixture.

title: Cold Ashby V Rothwell Manor
content:
What a difference a few months makes. After the disappointment of the reverse fixture earlier in the season which fell victim to the unpredictable British weather and had to be cancelled entirely, the return match at Cold Ashby was played in absolutely glorious conditions.

It proved to be an incredibly close and competitive afternoon of bowls, with both teams finding their rhythm on a fine green. Ultimately, Cold Ashby's strength across the rinks secured the day, with the home side taking the victory on two rinks, while the visitors claimed one.

When the final wood was delivered and the scores were tallied, Cold Ashby emerged victorious with a hard-earned 45 to 38 win.

A special mention goes to the afternoon's standout performance for the home side: the formidable trio of Rick, Dean, and Harry, who put on a superb display to finish as Cold Ashby's top winning rink.

Thank you to Rothwell Manor for a fantastic game played in great spirit, and well done to everyone involved.

title: Carlsberg V Cold Ashby
content:
Cold Ashby Secure Solid Away Win in Historical Northampton Clash.

Cold Ashby Bowls Club braved the pre-match elements to secure a convincing 46-35 shot victory against Carlsberg, played at their historic venue in Northampton. Despite challenging weather conditions, our team displayed resilience and skill throughout the match.

title: West Haddon V Cold Ashby
content: On Thursday we travelled the short distance to play at West Haddon. On a lovely summer evening all players enjoyed the match. Cold Ashby were in control for all the match, winning convincingly on the day.

title: Local Derby Delight as Cold Ashby Dominate South Kilworth
content: Cold Ashby made the short six-mile trip to near-neighbours South Kilworth this weekend, returning home with a convincing and hard-fought victory. It was a dominant performance from our side with excellent displays across all rinks.

title: Venue: Desborough (Away)
content:
Final Score: Desborough 71 – 47 Cold Ashby

It was a challenging afternoon on the road for Cold Ashby, as the visitors struggled to come to terms with some very difficult playing conditions at Desborough.

title: Cold Ashby v Geddington
content: Unfortunately this match was called off due to the weather

title: Rothwell Manor V Cold Ashby
content: Unfortunately this match was called off due to the weather

title: Bugbrooke v Cold Ashby
content:
Cold Ashby endured a tough afternoon on the road, facing not only a strong opponent but also punishingly hot and sultry conditions.

The elements played a major factor in the contest, with the extreme heat making it a grueling day for all players on the green.

title: Club Competitions & BBQ
content:
Our scheduled opponents unfortunately had to cancel as they were unable to field a team.

However, the blank Saturday did not go to waste. The afternoon was instead used to advantage with internal club competitions and a BBQ for members to enjoy.

title: Cold Ashby V Rugby Railway
content: On a bitterly cold evening, Cold Ashby entertained one of the strongest teams in the area. Despite the drop in temperature, the home side put up a gallant fight from the opening ends, showing character and determination throughout the match.

title: Whyte Melville V Cold Ashby
content: Cold Ashby traveled to Fernie Fields on Sunday—the home of Whyte Melville Bowls Club—for a closely contested match. Despite a forecast threatening rain, the weather held out, allowing for a competitive game on the day.

title: Harpole V Cold Ashby
content: Cold Ashby traveled to Harpole on Saturday for a closely contested encounter. While the final scoreline suggests a relatively tight margin of 16 shots, the narrative of the match was distinctly one-sided for much of the afternoon.

title: North Kilworth vs Cold Ashby
content: In a challenging away fixture, Cold Ashby fell short against a disciplined North Kilworth side. The story of the day was the heavy rink at North Kilworth requiring significant weight adjustment from our players.

title: Daventry V Cold Ashby
content:
That is a fantastic turnaround! Winning by 9 shots overall (84 to 75) shows a real team effort. A huge shout-out to Chris H, Harry, and Damo for leading the charge as the winning rink.

Match Highlights:
- The Comeback: Successfully snapped a two-game losing streak.
- Final Score: Cold Ashby 84 | Daventry 75
- Standout Performance: Chris, Harry, and Damo winning their rink.

title: Cold Ashby V L.V.A.
content:
Under a cloudless sky, Cold Ashby entertained L.V.A. in what proved to be a highly competitive afternoon. The home team ultimately lost narrowly by 56 shots to 51.

The standout performance came from the winning rink of Rick, Harry, and guest player Pete Ridley.

title: Club Competion Report. The Triples Cup
content:
Overview:
The season kicked off in spectacular fashion with the first of our internal club competitions, the Triples Cup. Players were treated to ideal conditions, competing under a clear blue sky.

The Match:
The tournament proved to be an exceptionally tight contest. Momentum shifted several times throughout the afternoon, with the battle for the trophy coming down to the final ends. In the end, only 3 shots separated the winners from the runners-up.

Results - Congratulations to our podium finishers:
Winning Team: Roger, Dean, & Pedro
Runners-up: Ollie, Matt, & Harry

A huge thank you to everyone who participated and helped make the first competition of the season such a success. We look forward to seeing this level of competition continue throughout the year!

title: Season Opener V Fleckney
content:
The bowls season kicked off under a clear sky for the opening match. While it was a fine but cool day, the crisp air didn't dampen the spirits of the players, and the green was in excellent condition.

The Result:
It was a nail-biting encounter that went down to the final woods. In a remarkably close contest, Cold Ashby lost by just one shot.
South Kilworth: 71 shots
Cold Ashby: 70 shots

Rink Highlights:
The home team's top-performing rink featured Chris, Stuart, and Damo, who secured a solid win for the club. However, one home rink suffered a heavy defeat of 26 shots to 6, creating a 20-shot deficit that proved difficult to overcome.

Summary:
Despite the heartbreaking one-shot loss, the quality of the green and the competitive nature of the game suggest a promising season ahead for Cold Ashby. With a bit more consistency across all rinks, that single-shot margin will surely swing in their favor next time.

title: Meet and Greet
content:
Members of Cold Ashby Bowls Club held their pre-season get together on April 11th 2026. There was an excellent turnout, with lots of interest shown from potential new members.

The event included the first roll up on the green, which is in superb condition. The first bowl of the season was bowled by Robert Carpenter. A great start to the 2026 season!
